Source_Species.reason: AcoCYP93A1 is described as from A. coreanum; Aco prefix and Results explicitly name AcoCYP93A1 among functional P450s from A. coreanum.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper uses the Latin name Aconitum coreanum.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional validation was performed by transient coexpression in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Nicotiana benthamiana is explicitly stated throughout Results/Methods.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Genes were cloned into pEAQ-HT vectors and analyzed by transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: AcoCYP93A1 contributes to production of oxidized ent-atiserene products/atiserenoids; the paper frames these as cryptic atiserenoids.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Heterologous production in N. benthamiana is supported, but the statement broadly saying 'Aconitum tissues and heterologous N. benthamiana production system' is only partially supported because specific AcoCYP93A1 products are not directly shown as accumulated in Aconitum tissues.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper reports some atiserenoids showed allelopathic root-growth inhibition activity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Candidate P450s were identified from coexpression analysis/SOM nodes with functional TPSs in A. coreanum.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The gene was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: AcoCYP93A1 is explicitly assigned to the CYP93A subfamily of cytochrome P450s.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Coexpression with CfDXS, AtGGPPS, AcaCPS and ent-atiserene biosynthesis genes is supported experimentally, and combination with CYP725A is discussed, but direct physical/protein interaction is not shown; these are pathway coexpression partners only.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Results include coexpression in N. benthamiana, GC-MS/LC-MS, large-scale purification with NMR, AlphaFold2 docking, and site-directed mutagenesis for AcoCYP93A1.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The paper states AcoCYP93A1 oxidizes ent-atiserene at three distinct sites to generate A8-A10, i.e. multifunctional oxidation producing diverse atiserenoids.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four summary points are directly supported: multifunctionality on ent-atiserene, three oxidation sites producing A8-A10, key residues I404/I241 from mutagenesis, and products contributing to atiserenoid chemical space with some atiserenoids showing allelopathy.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly states this is the first report of CYP93A family members oxidizing labdane-related diterpene scaffolds.

Omics_Data.reason: A. coreanum RNA-seq de novo transcriptome was used; BioProject PRJNA1187874 is given in Data availability.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper explicitly discusses promiscuity/mixed product profiles and improved specificity when coexpressed with partner P450s.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion supports use of these enzymes as toolkit parts for designer oxidation and synthetic biology diversification of diterpenoids.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: The gene was validated by transient coexpression in N. benthamiana with LC-MS/GC-MS, and products from pathway combinations were structurally characterized by NMR.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly state AcoCYP725A1 could oxidize ent-atiserene at four distinct sites and combinations with other P450s raised A13 or A15 yields.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: The statements match Results: highly multifunctional, generates A7/13/15-related products, and boosts dominant products in combinations.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state AcoCYP725A1 is derived from A. coreanum only and lacks an obvious homologous pair among the functional P450s.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The work concerns labdane-related diterpenoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: AcoTPS3 makes 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene, establishing the 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene branch toward these products.

Enzyme_Name.reason: AcoTPS3 is described as capable of generating 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene/16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurane synthase activity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: It was transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves.

Primary_Product.reason: Compound 4 was assigned as 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene and produced by AcoTPS3.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Results describe AcoTPS3 as a functional class I diTPS ionizing ent-CPP to yield 16α-hydroxy-ent-kaurene.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: This is the scaffold-forming upstream diterpene synthase step.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: The paper explicitly states guan-fu diterpenoid A and tripterifordin are oxidative products derived from 4.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Tripterifordin was detected in A. coreanum tuber.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Tripterifordin is described as having anti-HIV activity, and guan-fu diterpenoid A is described as a bioactive diterpenoid in context.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These genes were coexpressed together functionally (CfDXS, AtGGPPS, AcaCPS, AcaCYP71A1, Aco/AcaCYP701A2), but direct protein-protein interactions were not tested.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results state discovery of AcoTPS3 suggests 4 may be an alternative precursor scaffold and that such activity had not been found from Aconitum spp. before.

Omics_Data.reason: A. coreanum RNA-seq/de novo transcriptome and BioProject PRJNA1187874 are stated.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by transient expression in N. benthamiana, GC-MS, large-scale expression, and NMR structural elucidation of downstream products/compound 4.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The value is 'not established', which is consistent because the paper does not claim rate-limiting status.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: These genes were coexpressed in pathway reconstruction, but no direct physical interactions were shown.
